To own Spyre Therapeutics today, you need to believe that its inflammatory and autoimmune pipeline, especially the SKYLINE and SKYWAY programs, can ultimately justify a loss-making, pre-revenue business with an expensive valuation and a history of dilution. The recent US$500 million at-the-market facility and omnibus shelf are all about keeping those trials funded, but they also raise the prospect of further equity issuance on top of April’s US$403 million raise. data.title: Chief Technology Officer Of Immunovant Sold 53% Of Their Shares
data.description:
Some Immunovant, Inc. (NASDAQ:IMVT) shareholders may be a little concerned to see that the Chief Technology Officer, Jay Stout, recently sold a substantial US$1.5m worth of stock at a price of US$38.31 per share. Probably the most concerning element of the whole transaction is that the disposal amounted to 53% of their entire holding.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

Immunovant Insider Transactions Over The Last Year

In fact, the recent sale by Jay Stout was the biggest sale of Immunovant shares made by an insider individual in the last twelve months, according to our records. So we know that an insider sold shares at around the present share price of US$38.14. While we don't usually like to see insider selling, it's more concerning if the sales take place at a lower price. We note that this sale took place at around the current price, so it isn't a major concern, though it's hardly a good sign.

Insiders in Immunovant didn't buy any shares in the last year. You can see a visual depiction of insider transactions (by companies and individuals) over the last 12 months, below. If you click on the chart, you can see all the individual transactions, including the share price, individual, and the date!

View our latest analysis for Immunovant

If you like to buy stocks that insiders are buying, rather than selling, then you might just love this free list of companies. (Hint: Most of them are flying under the radar).

Does Immunovant Boast High Insider Ownership?

I like to look at how many shares insiders own in a company, to help inform my view of how aligned they are with insiders. A high insider ownership often makes company leadership more mindful of shareholder interests. Insiders own 0.8% of Immunovant shares, worth about US$63m. While this is a strong but not outstanding level of insider ownership, it's enough to indicate some alignment between management and smaller shareholders.

So What Do The Immunovant Insider Transactions Indicate?
